_meta.description: Regex and filename patterns used by the security_engineer agent's secret-scan pass over the repository working tree. Regex entries use kind='regex' and scan file contents; filename entries use kind='filename' and are matched against the output of `git ls-files`.

patterns.title: AWS access key ID
patterns.description: An AWS access key ID of the form AKIA followed by 16 base32 characters was found. Rotate the key immediately and audit CloudTrail for unauthorized use.

patterns.title: AWS secret access key
patterns.description: A 40-character base64-ish string assigned near an AWS-related identifier. False positives are possible; the security engineer verifies context before reporting.

patterns.title: Stripe secret key
patterns.description: A Stripe secret key (sk_test_* or sk_live_*) was found. Live keys must be rotated immediately via the Stripe dashboard.

patterns.title: Stripe publishable key
patterns.description: A Stripe publishable key (pk_test_* or pk_live_*) was found. Publishable keys are safe in client code, but live keys in test fixtures or example files are reported for cleanup.

patterns.title: Supabase service-role / JWT token
patterns.description: A JWT-shaped token (three base64url segments separated by dots) was found. Supabase service_role keys follow this shape and grant admin access; the security engineer filters by context (surrounding 'service_role' or SUPABASE_* identifiers) before raising a high-severity finding.

patterns.title: Private key material
patterns.description: A PEM-encoded private key header was found in the working tree. Private keys must never be committed; rotate immediately and remove from history.

patterns.title: Environment file tracked by git
patterns.description: An environment file that should be git-ignored is tracked. Verify against `git ls-files` rather than a working-tree scan; .env.example and .env.sprint.example are explicitly allowed.
